definition:
1. to long for, yearn after  what is absent, to miss  or regret  what is lost, Lat. desiderare, (Homer), etc.; ποθεῖς τὸν οὐ παρόντα (Aristophanes Comicus); π. τὰς ἐν τῆι νεότητι ἡδονάς (Plato Philosophus):—;Pass., ὦ ποθουμένη (i.e. Εἰρήνη) (Aristophanes Comicus) 
2. of things, to require , ποθεῖ ἡ ἀπόκρισις ἐρώτησιν τοιάνδε (Plato Philosophus) 
3. with infinitive to be anxious  to do, (Euripides); τὸ νοσοῦν ποθεῖ σε ξυμπαραστάτην λαβεῖν my sickness needs  to take thee as an assistant, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
4. absolute, τὸ ποθοῦν one's desiring, one's longing , (Sophocles Tragicus) 
5. as Dep., ποθουμένη φρήν the longing  soul, (Sophocles Tragicus) (ML)
